chapter_info.subject: Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: Mindful Eating: A Path to a Healthy Body

content.text: The phrase 'annena jtni jivanti' (Sanskrit) means 'food gives life to living beings.' Across India, food variety is shaped by soil types, climate, cultivation, culture, and traditions.

content.title: Activity 3.2: Regional Food Mapping
content.content: Traditional food is usually based on locally grown crops. For example: Punjab (Maize/Wheat crops -> Makki di roti), Karnataka (Rice/Ragi crops -> Idli/Dosa), and Manipur (Rice/Bamboo crops -> Eromba).

content.text: Cooking tools have changed due to technology. Traditional tools like 'chulha' and 'sil-batta' have been replaced by modern gas stoves and electrical grinders for ease and efficiency.
Carbohydrates are primary energy sources. Glucose provides instant energy. Cereals (wheat, rice), tubers (potato), and fruits are major sources.
Fats provide stored energy and keep us warm. Sources include oils, nuts (almonds, walnuts), and animal products (ghee, butter).

content.title: Example 1
content.description: Per 100g, potato wafers provide 536 kcal of energy while roasted chana provides 355 kcal. Why might chana be consider a healthier energy source?
content.solution: Although wafers provide more energy, it is mostly from high fat (35g vs 6.26g in chana). Chana provides more proteins (18.64g vs 7g) and fibre, making it a sustainable energy source.

content.text: Proteins are essential for growth and repair. Sources: Pulses, milk products, eggs, fish, and edible mushrooms. Growing children need higher protein servings.
Vitamins (A, B1, C, D) and Minerals (Iron, Calcium, Iodine) are needed in small amounts but protect the body from diseases.

content.title: Historical Case Studies
content.text: 1) Scurvy (bleeding gums) was common among sailors; cured by Vitamin C found in citrus fruits (James Lind's observation). 2) Goitre (neck swelling) was prevalent in Himalayan regions due to iodine deficiency in soil; solved by using iodised salt.
Lack of specific nutrients leads to disorders: Vitamin A (Night blindness), Vitamin C (Scurvy), Vitamin D/Calcium (Bone/Tooth decay), Iodine (Goitre), and Iron (Anemia/fatigue).

content.title: Example 2
content.description: A person has difficulty seeing in dim light. Suggest two food items they should add to their diet based on the textbook chart.
content.solution: They are suffering from Vitamin A deficiency. They should add milk, green vegetables, or fruits (like papaya/mango) as per Fig 3.5.

content.title: Nutrient Identification Protocol
content.content: 1) Starch: Iodine solution (Positive result: Blue-black colour). 2) Fats: Paper rub test (Positive result: Persistent oily patch). 3) Proteins: Copper sulphate + Caustic soda solution (Positive result: Violet colour).

content.text: A balanced diet provides all nutrients, roughage (fibre), and water in the right proportions. Junk food (chips, carbonated drinks) is high in calories but low in quality nutrients, leading to obesity.
Fortified foods have nutrients added during processing (e.g., iodised salt). FSSAI regulates these standards in India.
Native Indian crops like Jowar, Bajra, and Ragi are called 'nutri-cereals'. They are rich in vitamins, iron, calcium, and fibre, and can grow in diverse climates.
The distance food travels from farm to plate is called 'food miles'. Reducing these miles supports local farmers, ensures freshness, and reduces transport pollution.

content.title: Example 3
content.description: Compare consuming a local apple (10km travel) to an imported one (5000km travel). List two advantages of the local choice.
content.solution: 1) Lower food miles mean less transport pollution. 2) The local apple is likely fresher and healthier as it spent less time in transit.

glossary.definition: Components like carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, and minerals that support growth and health.
Dietary fibre that helps get rid of undigested food.
High-calorie foods deficient in proteins, minerals, and vitamins.
The process of adding extra nutrients to food during processing.
The distance travelled by a food item from the producer to the consumer.

summary_points: Food choices in India vary by region and locally grown crops.
Nutrients are divided into energy-giving (carbs, fats), body-building (proteins), and protective (vitamins, minerals).
Balanced diets include nutrients, roughage, and water in proper quantities.
Iodine, Vitamin C, and Calcium deficiencies lead to Goitre, Scurvy, and Rickets respectively.
Reducing food miles and avoiding wastage supports both personal health and the planet.
